excerpts.text: The teaching of the Lord of Heaven does not contradict the Way of Confucius. Rather, it completes what Confucius began and brings it to its fullest expression.
excerpts.source_title: Preface to The True Meaning of the Lord of Heaven

excerpts.text: I have studied the doctrines of the Western scholars and found them to be profoundly reasonable. Their mathematics, their astronomy, and their theology form a unified whole.
I was baptized in Nanjing by Father Ricci's associate. From that day, I knew that the Lord of Heaven was the source of all truth, both Eastern and Western.
Together with Father Ricci, I translated Euclid's Elements into Chinese. Geometry is the foundation of all learning, and China must master it.
excerpts.source_title: Preface to the Chinese translation of Euclid's Elements

excerpts.text: The calendar must be reformed. The old Chinese methods are no longer accurate. The Western methods are superior, and we must adopt them for the good of the empire.
The Western teaching has three virtues: it supplements Confucianism, it corrects Buddhism, and it surpasses Daoism.
excerpts.source_title: Bian Xue Zhang Shu (On Learning and Scholarship)

excerpts.text: I have translated works on hydraulics, agriculture, and military science from the Western missionaries. China must learn from the West if she is to prosper.
excerpts.source_title: Nong Zheng Quan Shu (Complete Treatise on Agricultural Administration)

excerpts.text: Those who attack the Western teaching do so out of ignorance. They have not read the books. They have not spoken with the scholars. They merely repeat the slanders of the envious.
excerpts.source_title: Defense of the Jesuits before the court

excerpts.text: My conversion was not hasty. I studied the doctrine for many months before I was baptized. Reason led me to faith, and faith confirmed what reason had found.
Father Ricci was the most learned man I have ever met. His knowledge of mathematics, astronomy, geography, and philosophy was vast. And yet he was humble as a child.
I serve the emperor as a loyal subject and I worship the Lord of Heaven as a faithful believer. These two duties do not conflict.
Some say that to follow the Western religion is to betray China. But the truth belongs to no nation. It belongs to all mankind.
In matters of mathematics and astronomy, the Westerners are far ahead of us. We must humbly learn from them, as they humbly learn our language and customs.
I have risen to the rank of Grand Secretary. I use my position not for personal gain, but to open the door for truth — both scientific and spiritual.
The Lord of Heaven created all things. He governs all things. He is not the Heaven of the philosophers, which is merely an impersonal principle. He is a living God.
I have introduced the sweet potato to northern China. If this crop takes root, it will save millions from famine. Science in the service of the people — this is my calling.
My family has followed me into the faith. My grandmother, my wife, my children — all have been baptized. The Lord of Heaven has blessed my household.
At court there are those who wish to expel the Western scholars. I have defended them before the emperor, for they bring knowledge that China needs.
The ancient sages of China knew of Shangdi, the Lord on High. The Western teaching restores what we have lost through centuries of Buddhist and Daoist corruption.
I die as a servant of two masters who are not in conflict: the Son of Heaven on earth, and the Lord of Heaven above. Both have received my faithful service.
